name: Dancing Israelis
description: The "Dancing Israelis" is a conspiracy theory that alleges a group of Israelis were observed celebrating and photographing the September 11, 2001 attacks. Proponents claim these individuals were agents of the Mossad (Israeli intelligence) and had prior knowledge of the attacks, suggesting Israeli involvement in the events. The theory originated shortly after 9/11, fueled by reports of five Israelis arrested in New Jersey on the day of the attacks. These individuals were employees of a moving company called Urban Moving Systems. Some witnesses claimed they saw the men celebrating and photographing the World Trade Center towers burning. The FBI investigated the incident but concluded that the individuals were not involved in the attacks, and the case was closed. The Israelis were eventually deported to Israel for visa violations. Mainstream media and law enforcement have dismissed the "Dancing Israelis" theory as baseless and anti-Semitic. They argue that the witness accounts were misinterpreted or exaggerated, and that the individuals were simply shocked and relieved to be alive after witnessing the attacks. Critics point to the lack of concrete evidence supporting the conspiracy theory and argue that it is rooted in anti-Israel sentiment and historical tropes about Jewish people controlling world events. The theory continues to circulate online and is often used to promote anti-Semitic views.
subtitle: Alleged Mossad agents celebrating 9/11 attacks.

claims.claim: A group of Israelis celebrated the 9/11 attacks.
claims.evidence: Witness accounts of Israelis celebrating and photographing the burning World Trade Center towers.
claims.criticism: Witness accounts are unreliable and potentially misinterpreted due to trauma and confusion. The FBI investigated and found no evidence of involvement in the attacks.

claims.claim: The Israelis were Mossad agents with prior knowledge of the attacks.
claims.evidence: The individuals worked for a moving company, Urban Moving Systems, which some believe was a Mossad front. The rapid deportation of the individuals is seen as a cover-up.
claims.criticism: No credible evidence links Urban Moving Systems to the Mossad. The deportation was due to visa violations, a common occurrence. The FBI investigation found no evidence of prior knowledge.

claims.claim: The 'Dancing Israelis' are evidence of Israeli involvement in 9/11.
claims.evidence: The alleged celebration and prior knowledge suggest a motive for Israeli involvement.
claims.criticism: The theory relies on speculation and anti-Semitic tropes. There is no credible evidence to support Israeli involvement in the 9/11 attacks. The official 9/11 Commission Report found no such evidence.

keyFigures.name: Christopher Bollyn
keyFigures.role: Prominent conspiracy theorist who promoted the 'Dancing Israelis' theory.

keyFigures.name: FBI
keyFigures.role: Investigated the incident and concluded there was no evidence of involvement in the 9/11 attacks.

keyFigures.name: Chip Berlet
keyFigures.role: Researcher of right-wing movements and conspiracy theories, who has critiqued the theory as antisemitic.

mythologyConnections: Protocols of the Elders of Zion

timeline.event: Five Israelis are arrested in New Jersey after witnesses report them celebrating and photographing the World Trade Center attacks.
The 'Dancing Israelis' conspiracy theory begins to circulate online.
FBI investigates the incident and closes the case, finding no evidence of involvement in the attacks. The Israelis are deported for visa violations.
